rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally involves assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and relationship to trust-region or self-feedback methods are coherently presented.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument from curvature feedback to descent quality to empirical usefulness follows logically.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about benchmark performance and overhead, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims match the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, the ordering and narrative progression across paragraphs are central review responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A section review commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, concision, and flow.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty around the broad instruction and the preliminary nature of the artifact's claims.
rationale.RX.2: The review scope requires guarding against stronger conclusions than the described evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must project responsibilities only and not perform the requested review or revise the section.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported optimization guidance.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should remain traceable to the delegation and the provided section text.
